hypothesis: Fine-tuning the eSEN model with a small, targeted DFT-labeled dataset of ~50 ML-converged SSNEB images from the CsPbI3 β→γ pathway using a loss function that weights stress components twice as strongly as energies and forces will reduce the number of VASP SSNEB restart iterations required for convergence by at least 50% and eliminate symmetry-breaking lattice distortions observed in VASP restarts compared to the pre-trained eSEN without fine-tuning.
